description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages (preliminaries, testing, deploy) and a set of variables defining build‑image tags and JOBS. Add a .FreeBSD job in the testing stage (tagged freebsd) that runs `git submodule update --init`, `sh autogen.sh`, `mkdir build && cd build`, `LIBS="-L/usr/local/lib" ../configure --disable-nls --without-root-tests`, `gmake -j$JOBS`, and `gmake check -j$JOBS`; it stores build/*.log and build/tests/*.log as on‑failure artifacts, runs only on branches of openconnect/ocserv and excludes tags and schedules. Add Centos7, Centos8, Centos9, Debian, Ubuntu20.04, Ubuntu22.04, Fedora, i386/Debian, minimal, minimal/Ubuntu20.04, musl/Alpine, asan/clang/Fedora, clang/Fedora, secsec/Fedora, ubsan/Fedora, static-analyzer/Fedora, noprocfs/distcheck/Fedora, codespell/Fedora, codingstyle/Fedora jobs that each run in the testing or preliminaries stage using their respective `$CI_REGISTRY/$BUILD_IMAGES_PROJECT` images, execute the same sequence of `git submodule update --init`, `autoreconf -fvi`, `./configure` (with job‑specific flags such as `--enable-code-coverage`, `CC=clang`, `CFLAGS` for sanitizers, or `--with-seccomp-trap`), `make -j$JOBS`, and `make check` (with optional VERBOSE, XFAIL_TESTS, or COVERAGE flags), and publish log files or scan‑build output as on‑failure artifacts. Include a Coverity job in the preliminaries stage that runs only on schedules or the coverity trigger, downloads the Coverity tool, configures with `CFLAGS="-g -O0"`, runs `cov-build` to produce `cov-int`, archives it, and uploads via curl, storing `cov-int/*.txt` as artifacts. Add a Signoff job in preliminaries that runs a script checking for missing Signed‑off‑by tags, a Triage job (schedules only) that installs the `gitlab-triage` gem and runs it with the project ID and API token, and a Pages job in the deploy stage (only on the master branch) that depends on the Fedora job, runs `make` commands to generate code‑coverage files into `public/coverage`, and publishes the `public` directory as the pages artifact.
